channel.title: Archival Science on The Modern Archivist
channel.description: Recent content in Archival Science on The Modern Archivist

item.title: The Ghost in the Archive: Detecting Semantic Decay in LLM-Generated Historiography
item.description:
The Problem of Synthetic Recursion

As large language models become embedded in the production pipeline of digital archives, a subtle but critical issue emerges: semantic decay. When AI-generated text is fed back into training corpora, the original nuance of historical records degrades with each iteration.

This phenomenon, which we term synthetic recursion, creates a feedback loop where the model’s own outputs gradually replace the source material it was meant to preserve.
